title: EFT: Alliance Work
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides for EFT alliance work.

sources.label: Watson and Sharbanee, Emotion Focused Therapy: Case Conceptualization and Treatment

slides.title: Marker: the process is between you
slides.body: Use alliance work when contact with you becomes the active emotional field.
bullets: Compliance, withdrawal, irritation, or confusion replaces exploration.
The client responds to task pressure more than felt process.
A rupture, misattunement, or fear of your reaction is live.

slides.title: Repair before you resume the task
slides.body: In EFT, the relationship is not a warm-up for technique; it is the condition that makes experiential work possible.
steps: Stop the task and name the process lightly: something changed between us.
Invite correction: what did I miss, push, assume, or not understand?
Validate the protective logic before asking for deeper contact.
Take responsibility for your part without making the client care for you.
Ask what would make the next minute safer or more useful.
Only return to task work when the client's body shows renewed consent.

slides.title: Experienced clinician tracking
rows: A missed meaning or dignity need
